reasoning: As of May 1, 2025, Kilmar Abrego Garcia remains in a Salvadoran maximum-security prison (CECOT). While the U.S. Supreme Court and a federal district judge have ordered the Trump administration to facilitate his return to the U.S., the administration has shown extreme defiance. On May 1, 2025, White House spokesperson Tricia McLaughlin explicitly stated, 'Kilmar Abrego Garcia will never be on American streets again,' and the administration has filed documents indicating potential plans to divert him to a third country (like Uganda) rather than return him to the U.S. Given the physical detention in El Salvador (whose government also opposes his return) and the explicit refusal of the U.S. executive branch to comply with court orders before the July 1 deadline, the probability of him entering U.S. territory in the next 60 days is very low. Physical return requires executive cooperation which is currently being withheld.
